scope_boundary: synthetic constitutional-predicate record discipline and finite Lean alignment predicates only; no deployed constitutional alignment, moral correctness, runtime policy behavior, source interpretation, self-modification safety, predicate-translation adequacy, review quality, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has a constitutional-predicate schema, a protocol fixture, finite Alignment Lean predicates, proof adequacy review text that keeps the target narrow, exact source mappings for the Constitutional Alignment chapter, and a synthetic constitutional-alignment harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures. Those artifacts make protected scope, operational tests, conflict routing, review routes, self-modification weakening rules, migration policies, least-sufficient-power behavior, uncertainty preservation, and non-claim boundaries executable at the record level. They still do not establish deployed constitutional alignment, moral correctness, runtime policy behavior, source interpretation adequacy, self-modification safety, predicate-translation adequacy, review quality, or whole-system alignment.
required_artifacts: deployed constitutional policy engine or constraint compiler over real plans
reviewed predicate-translation corpus with positive and negative controls
runtime trace showing protected predicates narrow, defer, block, or route actual work
self-modification safety test over actual predicate migration and rollback behavior
least-sufficient-power evaluation over real tool, memory, planning, or self-improvement choices
independent moral, governance, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_constitutional_alignment.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: schema validation checks record shape rather than constitutional adequacy
finite Lean predicates remain useful but too narrow for deployed constitutional alignment, self-modification safety, or moral-correctness claims
predicate-translation adequacy, review quality, runtime policy behavior, source interpretation review, and deployed least-sufficient-power enforcement are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as deployed constitutional-alignment evidence
synthetic predicate fixtures interpreted as predicate-translation adequacy or review-quality evidence
finite Lean alignment predicates treated as deployed runtime policy behavior
least-sufficient-power fixture interpreted as actual tool, memory, planning, or self-improvement enforcement
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic predicate discipline that passed, or after deployed constitutional constraint compilation, predicate-translation review, runtime protected-predicate traces, self-modification migration and rollback tests, least-sufficient-power evaluations, and independent moral, governance, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove that real plans are constitutionally constrained
schema validation does not prove moral correctness or predicate adequacy
finite Lean predicates do not implement a constitutional policy engine
review-route strings do not prove reviewer independence or review quality
least-sufficient-power strings do not prove runtime route selection or power minimization
no independent moral, governance, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent moral-philosophy, governance, runtime-systems, reviewer-quality, formal-methods, self-modification, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no deployed constitutional policy engine or constraint compiler
no runtime protected-predicate trace over real work
no self-modification migration or rollback test
no least-sufficient-power evaluation over real choices
no independent moral, governance, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review
